status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

pivot_note: 2026-07-14: project rewritten around docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md (single farm, 4 heterogeneous Farm Assets, isometric digital twin, AI-Copilot-centric UI, structured 6-field recommendations). This replaces the prior rice-cooperative build (feat-001 through feat-007, formerly all 'passing'). That build's full evidence trail is preserved in progress.md under 'Legacy: rice-cooperative build (superseded 2026-07-14)' -- nothing was deleted, the roadmap moved forward. See docs/architecture.md and docs/ui-build-plan.md for the current target design.

features.name: Rebuild Snowflake schema for the FarmTwin asset model
features.description: Via CoCo, replace FARMS/SENSOR_READINGS/RISK_ASSESSMENTS/WORK_ORDERS/CROP_HISTORY with FARM_ASSETS, ASSET_READINGS, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, RECOMMENDATIONS, ASSET_HISTORY per the schema table in docs/architecture.md. WEATHER_READINGS is kept but becomes farm-wide (no farm_id). Seed FARM_ASSETS with exactly 4 rows (fish_pond, chicken_coop, rice_field, fruit_orchard) and generate realistic evolving time-series seed data for ASSET_READINGS/ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS/ASSET_HISTORY per the vision doc's per-asset simulated-data list.
features.user_visible_behavior: Querying the new tables in Snowsight returns the expected schema and seed rows for exactly 4 assets.
verification: Every new prompt added to snowflake/coco-prompts.md has its 'Result' line filled in
SELECT * FROM FARM_ASSETS returns exactly 4 rows, one per asset_type
SELECT * from each new table returns non-empty, type-appropriate seed rows without error
evidence: 2026-07-14: User ran both Part 2 CoCo prompts (schema rebuild + seed data) against the live account; results recorded in snowflake/coco-prompts.md with 'Result' lines filled in. Old FARMS/SENSOR_READINGS/RISK_ASSESSMENTS/WORK_ORDERS/CROP_HISTORY dropped; FARM_ASSETS/ASSET_READINGS/ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS/RECOMMENDATIONS/ASSET_HISTORY created; WEATHER_READINGS redefined farm-wide.

2026-07-14: Independently verified via backend/venv against the live account (not just trusting CoCo's report): FARM_ASSETS=4 rows (CC-001 chicken_coop, FO-001 fruit_orchard, FP-001 fish_pond, RF-001 rice_field, each with grid_x/grid_y/install_date populated), ASSET_READINGS=120 rows (30 days x 4 assets, type-specific columns populated and irrelevant columns NULL per asset type), ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS=22 rows (confirmed the fish pond's dissolved-oxygen critical escalation: 4.8 -> 3.8 -> 3.4 -> 3.1 mg/L across the last few days, risk_level='critical'), ASSET_HISTORY=12 rows (confirmed FP-001's Q4-2024 biomass crash 310kg -> 145kg tied to a prior DO event). RECOMMENDATIONS=0 and WEATHER_READINGS=0 rows, both expected (populated by /workflow/run in feat-012, not seed data). DESCRIBE TABLE RECOMMENDATIONS confirmed all 12 planned columns exist with the right types (recommendation_id/asset_id are VARCHAR, not numeric, unlike the old WORK_ORDER_ID -- noted for feat-010's ID-generation design).
features.notes: Prerequisite for every other feature below. This was a destructive rebuild of the live OPS schema (dropped the old rice-farm tables) -- run interactively via CoCo by the user per CLAUDE.md. asset_id/recommendation_id are string codes (e.g. 'FP-001'), not auto-incrementing numbers -- backend ID generation for RECOMMENDATIONS needs a different strategy than the old MAX(WORK_ORDER_ID)+1 hack (e.g. a generated string like '{asset_id}-REC-{timestamp}' or a UUID).

features.name: Rebuild semantic view + Cortex Agent for structured, asset-aware recommendations
features.description: Via CoCo, rebuild the semantic view over FARM_ASSETS/ASSET_READINGS/ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS/RECOMMENDATIONS/ASSET_HISTORY, then rebuild the Cortex Agent so its responses can be parsed into the 6-field Recommendation/Reason/Evidence/Priority/Expected Impact/Confidence format mandated by docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md, per asset, instead of one shared free-text narrative.
features.user_visible_behavior: Asking the agent about a specific asset returns a response containing all 6 required fields, grounded in that asset's real Snowflake data.
verification: A test prompt against the rebuilt agent returns a response containing all 6 fields for a specific asset
The semantic view answers at least 3 verified onboarding-style questions correctly (mirroring the rigor of the original FARM_OPS_VIEW verification)
evidence: 2026-07-14: User ran both remaining Part 2 CoCo prompts (semantic view + Cortex Agent) against the live account; results in snowflake/coco-prompts.md. Semantic view CLIMATE_AG_COPILOT.OPS.FARM_OPS_VIEW rebuilt over all 5 new tables, 3 verified onboarding queries passed ('which assets need attention today' correctly returned the fish pond's critical DO alert; 'current status of the fish pond' matched the latest reading; the history-comparison query correctly joined ASSET_HISTORY). Cortex Agent rebuilt in-place as CLIMATE_AG_COPILOT.OPS.FARM_OPS_AGENT (same name/schema as before, so backend/app/services/cortex_agent_client.py needed no code change) with response instructions enforcing the exact 6-field format.

2026-07-14: Independently verified by calling ask_agent()-equivalent directly against the live agent (not just trusting CoCo's report) with 'Assess the fish pond (FP-001) current condition and give your recommendations.' Got a real, grounded response: identified FP-001 as a critical DO emergency, cited exact current sensor values (DO 3.5 mg/L, water temp 32.9C, biomass 319 kg) and the full Jul 6-12 DO decline trend, correctly cross-referenced the Q4-2024 historical biomass crash (310 -> 145 kg) from ASSET_HISTORY, and produced 4 distinct recommendations -- every one containing all 6 required fields (Recommendation/Reason/Evidence/Priority/Expected Impact/Confidence) with specific data citations, not generic advice. Confidence values varied sensibly per recommendation (95%/88%/80%/70%). Hit one transient httpx.ReadTimeout on the first call (known flaky behavior from the prior build, see feat-006 notes); succeeded on retry with a longer client-side timeout -- not a regression in the rebuilt agent.
features.notes: Agent emits structured-but-parseable prose (markdown with bolded field labels), not structured JSON -- backend parsing in feat-012 will need to extract the 6 fields from this format rather than json.loads(). The prior build's ReadTimeout flakiness on cold Cortex Agent calls persists; keep backend/app/services/cortex_agent_client.py's 90s timeout (or increase it) when feat-012 wires this in.

features.name: New Pydantic models + Snowflake read/write layer for the asset schema
features.description: Replace backend/app/models/schemas.py's Plot/PlotRisk/WorkOrder/RiskAssessment/DailyBriefing/BriefingToday with FarmAsset, AssetReading, AssetRisk, Recommendation (6-field), DailyBriefing v2, BriefingToday v2. Add the corresponding query/insert/update functions in snowflake_client.py usage sites for the new tables.
features.user_visible_behavior: No visible behavior change yet (no endpoints wired) -- this is the typed foundation the next features build on.
verification: cd backend && python -m compileall app
A standalone script (or Python REPL) using the new models successfully runs a SELECT against FARM_ASSETS and ASSET_READINGS and constructs valid FarmAsset/AssetReading instances from the rows
evidence: 2026-07-14: Rewrote backend/app/models/schemas.py: FarmAsset, AssetReading (15 nullable type-specific fields matching ASSET_READINGS exactly), AssetRisk, AssetHistory, Recommendation (6-field: recommendation/reason/evidence/priority/expected_impact/confidence_pct, plus status/approved_by/approved_at), ApprovalRequest, DailyBriefing v2 (assets_assessed/high_risk_assets/recommendations_created), BriefingToday v2 (approved_recommendations/rejected_recommendations). Removed the old Plot/PlotRisk/WorkOrder/RiskAssessment models entirely.

2026-07-14: Removed the dead /plots, /plots/{id}/risk, /workorders/{id}/approve, /workorders/{id}/reject endpoints and their helper functions (_overall_risk_level, _work_order_from_row, _set_work_order_status) from backend/app/main.py -- their backing tables (FARMS, WORK_ORDERS) no longer exist post-feat-008. Stubbed /workflow/run and /briefing/today with explicit TODO(feat-011/012/013) comments and placeholder DailyBriefing/BriefingToday v2 responses, matching this repo's existing stub convention (see CLAUDE.md's note about the original /workflow/run stub).
2026-07-14: cd backend && python -m compileall app -- clean.

2026-07-14: Ran a standalone script against the live account (not just a dry construction) that SELECTs real rows from FARM_ASSETS (all 4), ASSET_READINGS (latest FP-001 row), ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS (latest FP-001 row), and ASSET_HISTORY (all 3 FP-001 periods), constructing FarmAsset/AssetReading/AssetRisk/AssetHistory instances from each -- all succeeded with no validation errors, field values matched the raw rows exactly (e.g. AssetReading correctly left all 10 irrelevant columns None for a fish_pond row).

2026-07-14: Started uvicorn against the live account (checked port 8000 was clear first, killed the process afterward) and curled all 3 live routes: GET /health -> {"status":"ok"}, POST /workflow/run and GET /briefing/today both returned valid DailyBriefing v2/BriefingToday v2 JSON matching the new schema with clear 'stubbed pending feat-0XX' summaries -- confirms the app boots cleanly with zero import errors against the new models (the actual risk this feature could have introduced, since main.py's old imports referenced now-deleted schema classes).
features.notes: Old Plot/PlotRisk/WorkOrder models and the /plots, /plots/{id}/risk, /workorders/{id}/approve|reject endpoints are fully removed (not just deprecated) since their backing tables were already dropped in feat-008 -- leaving them would have made main.py reference dead tables. /workflow/run and /briefing/today are now stubbed (not real) until feat-011/012/013 land; this is expected, tracked, incomplete-by-design state, not a regression.

features.name: Simulation engine for per-asset sensor data
features.description: No physical IoT exists. Build a simulation step (module under backend/app/services/, e.g. asset_simulator.py) that generates realistic, evolving values for each asset type's ASSET_READINGS columns on every /workflow/run call -- values should trend (not be purely random each run) so risk stories are visible over multiple runs, per docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md's 'simulated data should behave realistically over time.'
features.user_visible_behavior: Each POST /workflow/run call (once wired in feat-012) writes one new ASSET_READINGS row per asset, and values drift plausibly run over run rather than jumping randomly.
verification: cd backend && python -m compileall app
Calling the simulator function directly across 5+ simulated ticks for one asset shows a plausible trend (e.g. dissolved oxygen trending down, not random noise) matching a hand-picked scenario
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added backend/app/services/asset_simulator.py: next_reading(asset_type, previous) -- a persisted-last-value + bounded random walk per numeric metric (metric-specific min/max/step/drift), plus dedicated logic for growth_stage (ordered, one-step-at-a-time advance with 15% chance per tick), irrigation_status (derived from soil_moisture_pct), and egg_count (small random walk around the previous count). dissolved_oxygen_mg_l gets one extra directional-drift rule (continues declining below 4.0, gently recovers above 6.0, neutral in between) so the seeded fish-pond crisis continues realistically rather than randomly bouncing back to healthy.
2026-07-14: cd backend && python -m compileall app -- clean.

2026-07-14: Ran a 4-scenario verification script (seeded RNG for reproducibility): (A) fish_pond starting from the real seeded critical DO value (3.5 mg/L) across 8 ticks -- DO declined continuously (3.5 -> 3.24 -> 2.88 -> 2.49 -> 2.38 -> 2.15 -> 2.0, then held at the 2.0 floor), every tick-to-tick delta <= 0.39 (bounded, not random jumps), confirming a plausible worsening trend matching the demo's fish-pond crisis narrative; (B) fish_pond starting from a healthy DO value (7.0) stayed healthy/rose slightly over 5 ticks, confirming stable assets don't spuriously degrade; (C) rice_field growth_stage advanced exactly one stage at a time or held over 20 ticks (seedling -> vegetative -> reproductive -> ripening -> harvest_ready), never skipped or reversed; (D) chicken_coop egg_count/feed_level/water_l all evolved smoothly tick to tick with no implausible jumps.
features.notes: Kept the trend logic simple per the notes below: a persisted last-value + bounded random walk for every metric, plus one scripted directional rule (dissolved_oxygen_mg_l) tied to the specific demo scenario already present in the seed data -- not a general stress-detection framework across all metrics.

features.name: Rewrite /workflow/run as Observe -> Understand -> Recommend -> Predict
features.description: Replace run_daily_workflow's rice-farm logic with the 4-stage cycle per asset: Observe (run the feat-011 simulator + ingest farm-wide weather), Understand (write ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS per asset via the feat-009 agent), Recommend (write RECOMMENDATIONS rows in the full 6-field structured format), Predict (a short-horizon prediction, stored either as an ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS row with a future-looking risk_type or a dedicated notes field -- decide during implementation and record the decision in docs/architecture.md).
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /workflow/run writes real ASSET_READINGS, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, and RECOMMENDATIONS rows for all 4 assets and returns a DailyBriefing v2 reflecting real counts, not hardcoded values.
verification: uvicorn app.main:app --reload, then curl -X POST localhost:8000/workflow/run
New rows appear in ASSET_READINGS, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, and RECOMMENDATIONS for all 4 assets with today's timestamp
At least one RECOMMENDATIONS row has all 6 fields populated with non-placeholder content
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added backend/app/services/risk_engine.py (assess_risk: rule-based Understand stage using the same thresholds the agent itself was instructed to use -- DO<3.5=critical/<5.0=high/<6.0=medium, water temp>32C, disease>40%/20%, etc. -- across all 4 asset types; predict_trend: Predict stage, compares the new reading to the previous one and returns a one-sentence linear projection only when the driving metric is moving in the worsening direction) and backend/app/services/recommendation_parser.py (parse_recommendations: line-based regex extraction of the agent's 6 bolded field labels into structured dicts, tolerant of surrounding formatting variance).

2026-07-14: Rewrote backend/app/main.py's /workflow/run as Observe (simulate + write ASSET_READINGS via feat-011's simulator; fetch + write farm-wide WEATHER_READINGS, extended weather_client.get_today_reading to also pull wind_speed_10m_max) -> Understand (risk_engine.assess_risk, write ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS) -> Recommend (for medium+ risk assets only: real FARM_OPS_AGENT call with an asset-specific prompt, parsed into RECOMMENDATIONS rows) -> Predict (risk_engine.predict_trend, written as an ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS row with risk_type suffixed _forecast_24h). Added settings.farm_lat/farm_lon to config.py since FARM_ASSETS has no lat/lon post-pivot (one physical farm, not many geo-distributed plots) -- weather now needs a single configured location.

2026-07-14: Verified parse_recommendations() against the real 4-recommendation FP-001 response captured in feat-009's evidence (reconstructed as a fixture) -- correctly extracted all 4 blocks with all 6 fields each, matching priorities/confidences exactly.

2026-07-14: Ran /workflow/run against the live account 3 times while iterating (uvicorn started/stopped cleanly each time, port 8000 checked first). Run 1 (HTTP 200, 89.6s): assets_assessed=4, high_risk_assets=['FP-001'] (only the seeded crisis asset flagged, as intended), 3 RECOMMENDATIONS rows written with real distinct priority/confidence/reasoning text -- but the summary field leaked the agent's raw tool-call narration ('I'll assess... Let me query...') ahead of an inconsistent <answer> tag. Run 2 hit a genuine httpx.ReadTimeout at the 90s client timeout (same known Cortex Agent flakiness documented in feat-006/feat-009) -- bumped cortex_agent_client.py's timeout 90s->150s, same fix pattern as the prior build. Fixed the narration leak by discovering the wrapper format isn't consistent across calls (sometimes an explicit <answer> tag, sometimes narration runs straight into the first markdown heading with no tag) -- rewrote _clean_agent_answer() to handle both shapes plus a no-heading fallback, verified against 3 fixture shapes (tagged, untagged-with-heading, plain) all correct. Run 3 (HTTP 200, 100.4s) and run 4 (HTTP 200, 100.4s) both produced clean summaries starting directly at the structured report, zero narration leakage.

2026-07-14: Cross-checked Snowflake directly after run 4: ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS for FP-001 showed correctly alternating current (dissolved_oxygen, critical) and forecast (dissolved_oxygen_forecast_24h, critical, real linear-projection text) rows each tick; RECOMMENDATIONS accumulated 9 total rows across the 3 successful runs (3 per run, all for FP-001, all pending_approval); table counts grew as expected (ASSET_READINGS 120->135, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS 22->40, WEATHER_READINGS 0->4).
features.notes: This is the direct replacement for the old feat-002/003/004 pipeline, now covering 4 heterogeneous assets in one pass instead of N identical rice farms. Known scope limitation (not fixed, matching this repo's precedent of documenting rather than over-engineering around hackathon-scale limits): the per-asset loop has no cross-asset transaction/rollback -- if the agent call for one at-risk asset fails, assets later in ASSET_ID order that haven't been processed yet simply get caught up on the next /workflow/run call rather than the whole run atomically failing. Each Cortex Agent call is real and can take 60-150s; with only one asset at risk at a time (per the demo's 'one dramatic risk event' scope) this stays within a single request, but N simultaneous at-risk assets would serialize to N times that -- acceptable for this hackathon's one-asset-crisis demo, not for a general multi-incident production workflow.

features.name: Asset read endpoints + recommendation approve/reject + dashboard summary + briefing
features.description: Add GET /assets (list with latest health/status/risk), GET /assets/{id} (readings + risk + history), GET /assets/{id}/recommendations (structured cards), POST /recommendations/{id}/approve and /reject (write-back to RECOMMENDATIONS), GET /dashboard/summary (farm health score, active alerts derived from ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, tasks due today derived from pending RECOMMENDATIONS, weather, top recommendations, asset overview), and rebuild GET /briefing/today against RECOMMENDATIONS instead of WORK_ORDERS. Remove the old /plots, /plots/{id}/risk, /workorders/{id}/approve|reject endpoints.
features.user_visible_behavior: Every endpoint in docs/ui-build-plan.md's data contract table (except /copilot/ask) returns real Snowflake-backed data; approving/rejecting a recommendation flips its status and it disappears from the pending list and dashboard's Tasks Due Today.
verification: curl GET /assets, /assets/{id}, /assets/{id}/recommendations, /dashboard/summary, /briefing/today all return real Snowflake-backed data
POST /recommendations/{id}/approve and /reject update the row and are reflected in a subsequent GET
GET /assets/999/... style calls for a non-existent id return 404
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added AssetOverview/AssetDetail/AssetStatusSummary/DashboardSummary to schemas.py. Added GET /assets (latest non-forecast risk per asset via LEFT JOIN + QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER, health_score/status derived from risk_level via a fixed severity->score/status mapping), GET /assets/{id} (404 if missing; latest reading + latest current risk + latest _forecast_24h prediction row + full ASSET_HISTORY), GET /assets/{id}/recommendations (pending_approval only, 404 if asset missing), POST /recommendations/{id}/approve and /reject (404 if missing), GET /dashboard/summary (farm_health_score = average of per-asset health scores, active_alerts = latest high/critical non-forecast risk rows, tasks_due_today = all pending recommendations, top_recommendations = same list sorted by priority then confidence, real latest WEATHER_READINGS row). Rebuilt GET /briefing/today against RECOMMENDATIONS (was stubbed since feat-010).

2026-07-14: Ran uvicorn against the live account (port 8000 checked/cleared each restart) and curled every new route. GET /assets returned all 4 assets with correct risk_level/health_score/status (FP-001 correctly critical/10/critical from the real seeded crisis, the other 3 healthy/90). GET /assets/FP-001 returned the correct latest_reading, latest_risk, prediction (the _forecast_24h row), and all 3 ASSET_HISTORY periods. GET /assets/DOES-NOT-EXIST correctly 404'd. GET /assets/FP-001/recommendations returned all 9 pending recommendations accumulated across feat-012's test runs, each with real distinct 6-field content. GET /dashboard/summary's farm_health_score was exactly (90+90+10+90)/4=70 as expected, active_alerts contained only FP-001's real critical row, top_recommendations correctly priority+confidence sorted.

2026-07-14: POST /recommendations/{id}/approve (custom approved_by) and /reject (default approved_by) both updated status/approved_by/approved_at and returned the updated row; POST to a non-existent id correctly 404'd. GET /briefing/today (which now makes a real Cortex Agent call since approvals exist) returned approved_recommendations=[the approved one] and rejected_recommendations=[the rejected one], both matching exactly.

2026-07-14: Found a real bug during this verification: /briefing/today's summary leaked agent tool-planning narration in a 3rd shape _clean_agent_answer (written in feat-012) didn't yet handle -- no <answer> tag and no markdown heading at all, just narration sentences joined with zero separating whitespace ('assets.I'll query...') flowing directly into the real answer. Fixed by adding _strip_narration_prefix(): repeatedly cuts leading sentences starting with a small set of tool-planning lead-ins ('I'll ', 'Let me ', etc.) at the first sentence-ending punctuation, looping until the remaining text no longer starts with one. Verified against the exact captured real text (correctly stripped to the real answer) and a negative case (a normal sentence containing a decimal number, e.g. 'DO is at 3.5 mg/L... 3.5.', correctly left untouched). Re-verified live against a fresh /briefing/today call: summary now starts directly at the real content with zero narration leakage.
features.notes: Direct replacement for old feat-004/006's endpoint set, generalized from farms/plots to heterogeneous assets. _clean_agent_answer now handles 3 observed response shapes (tagged, heading-prefixed, bare narration) -- still a heuristic, not a guarantee, since the agent's exact wrapping isn't contractually stable; acceptable for this hackathon's demo scope.

features.name: AI Copilot free-form Q&A endpoint
features.description: Add POST /copilot/ask: takes a free-form question, calls the Cortex Agent grounded in current farm state via the semantic view, and returns an answer that ends with actionable next steps per the vision doc's 'Decision Intelligence' principle. Must handle the example questions in docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md verbatim (e.g. 'What should I do today?', 'Should I feed the fish?').
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /copilot/ask with a natural-language farm question returns a grounded, non-generic answer referencing real current asset state.
verification: curl -X POST /copilot/ask with each of the vision doc's example questions returns a response that references specific, real asset data (not generic agricultural advice)
Response text ends with a concrete next step, not just an observation
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added CopilotQuestion/CopilotAnswer to schemas.py and POST /copilot/ask to main.py -- takes a free-form question, wraps it with grounding + 'end with a concrete next step' instructions, calls FARM_OPS_AGENT, cleans the response with the same _clean_agent_answer used by /workflow/run and /briefing/today.

2026-07-14: Tested live against the real account with 3 of the vision doc's example questions (representative sample across question shapes -- broad daily-priority, asset-specific yes/no, farm-wide health summary -- not all 8, to keep verification time reasonable given each call takes 40-100s): 'What should I do today?' -> correctly triaged all 4 assets by urgency (FP-001 critical with full 6-field recommendation, FO-001 noted as harvest-ready with a real medium-priority harvest recommendation the simulator's growth_stage progression produced during feat-011/012 testing, CC-001/RF-001 correctly called healthy with real supporting data), ending with a concrete next step naming the exact already-approved action. 'Should I feed the fish?' -> correctly answered 'No', grounded in the real DO trend and Q4-2024 history, ending with a concrete next step. 'How healthy is the farm?' -> correctly summarized all 4 assets by health status with real data, ending with a concrete next step. All 3 responses matched the vision doc's explicit good-vs-bad example bar (grounded in real current state, not generic advice).

2026-07-14: Found and fixed a 4th narration-leak shape during this verification: 'I have the data model. Let me pull...' -- the opening sentence didn't match any of _clean_agent_answer's existing lead-in phrases (from feat-012/013), so the narration-stripping loop never started. Added 'I have the ', 'I have a ', "I've got the " to the lead-in list, verified against the exact captured text (correctly stripped to 'No — do not feed the fish...'), then re-ran the 'How healthy is the farm?' question live -- confirmed zero narration leakage.
features.notes: This is the doc's clearest anti-pattern check: 'Rice generally needs watering' (bad) vs. a response grounded in this farm's actual current state (good). All 3 tested responses cleared that bar. _clean_agent_answer's narration-lead-in list has now been extended 3 times across feat-012/013/014 as new real response shapes were observed live -- this remains a best-effort heuristic, not a contractual guarantee, since the agent's exact wrapping format isn't stable across calls.

features.name: Digital Twin home -- isometric farm map (replaces the real Leaflet/OSM Screen 1)
features.description: Build Screen 1 per docs/ui-build-plan.md: isometric 2D layout with one interactive object per asset positioned via grid_x/grid_y, color-coded green/yellow/red by risk level, hover popover (name/health/status/latest alert), click navigates to the asset detail screen (feat-017). Removes frontend/components/FarmMap.tsx (Leaflet) and its next/dynamic wiring in app/page.tsx.
features.user_visible_behavior: Opening localhost:3000 shows 4 isometric farm-asset objects (not a real-world map), color-coded by risk, with working hover and click-through.
verification: npm run build && npm run lint succeed in frontend/
Manual/Playwright walkthrough: load /, confirm all 4 assets render with correct colors matching GET /assets, hover shows the popover, click navigates to the correct asset detail page
evidence: 2026-07-14: Decided the open rendering question: plain CSS (absolute-positioned diamond tiles via clip-path + a standard 2:1 isometric coordinate transform from grid_x/grid_y), no library -- matches the prior build's bias toward minimal new dependencies. New frontend/components/DigitalTwinMap.tsx: 11x11 diamond ground grid, one marker per asset (emoji icon in a colored ring: emerald/amber/red for healthy/needs_attention/critical), hover popover (name/health_score/status/latest_alert), click -> Link to /assets/{id}.

2026-07-14: Rewrote frontend/lib/api.ts entirely for the new backend contract (AssetOverview/AssetDetail/AssetReading/AssetRisk/AssetHistory/Recommendation/DashboardSummary/BriefingToday v2/CopilotAnswer + matching fetch functions), replacing the dead Plot/WorkOrder types and functions (their backend endpoints no longer exist post-feat-010/013). Removed frontend/components/FarmMap.tsx and frontend/app/plots/[id]/page.tsx (both dead -- referenced removed API functions/endpoints). Ran `npm uninstall leaflet react-leaflet @types/leaflet`. Rewrote app/page.tsx as the Digital Twin home. Did a minimal compat patch (field renames only, not a redesign) to app/briefing/page.tsx so the build stays green against the new BriefingToday/Recommendation shape -- the real Screen 5 rebuild is feat-019's job.

2026-07-14: npm run build failed once on a stale generated .next/dev/types file still referencing the deleted /plots/[id] route (leftover Next.js typegen cache from a prior `next dev` session, not a real code error) -- cleared .next/ and rebuilt clean. npm run build and npm run lint both clean after.

2026-07-14: Playwright (chromium, installed fresh into the session scratchpad) walkthrough against live `uvicorn` + `next dev`: first attempt against http://127.0.0.1:3000 got 0 markers and a stuck 'Loading farm...' -- root-caused to a CORS mismatch (backend's allow_origins defaults to http://localhost:3000, Playwright was on the 127.0.0.1 origin); switched to http://localhost:3000 and all 4 asset markers rendered with correct hrefs (/assets/CC-001, FO-001, FP-001, RF-001). Hovering FP-001 showed the real live alert text ('Dissolved oxygen at 2.69 mg/L, below the 3.5 mg/L critical threshold.') and confirmed its ring uses the critical/red class; a healthy asset's hover showed 'Health score: 90 / Healthy'. Zero console/page errors (aside from expected dev-mode HMR websocket noise).

2026-07-14: Found and fixed a real bug during this walkthrough: a hovered marker's hover tooltip could render partially behind a neighboring marker with a higher base z-index (grid-position-derived), clipping tooltip text. Fixed by bumping the hovered asset's z-index to 500 (above all others) only while hovered. Re-verified: tooltip now renders fully on top with no clipping, confirmed via screenshot.

2026-07-14: Verified click-through with an explicit navigation wait (a first quick check without one looked like it hadn't navigated, which turned out to be a test-script race, not an app bug): clicking the FP-001 marker correctly navigates to /assets/FP-001, which correctly 404s via Next.js's built-in not-found page (expected -- that route doesn't exist until feat-017) with no crash and the nav bar still intact.
features.notes: Chose plain CSS/SVG over a library for the isometric rendering (see evidence). Leaflet/react-leaflet/@types/leaflet fully removed. /assets/{id} 404s until feat-017 lands -- expected, incremental, tracked state, not a regression. app/briefing/page.tsx got only a minimal compat patch, not its real redesign (feat-019).

features.name: Farm Dashboard screen
features.description: Build Screen 2 per docs/ui-build-plan.md: overall farm health score, active alerts, tasks due today, farm statistics, weather + forecast, daily recommendations (top N, priority-sorted), asset status overview. Reads GET /dashboard/summary.
features.user_visible_behavior: A dashboard screen answers 'how is my farm doing today' within a few seconds of loading, with real counts and a real weather forecast.
verification: npm run build && npm run lint succeed in frontend/
Manual walkthrough: load the dashboard route, confirm every displayed number/badge matches a direct curl of GET /dashboard/summary
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added frontend/components/RecommendationCard.tsx (reusable 6-field recommendation card, per ui-build-plan.md's 'reuse one recommendation-card component across screens' guidance) and frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx (new route): farm health score, active alerts count/list, tasks due today count, weather, daily recommendations (top 5), asset status overview grid. Added a Dashboard nav link in layout.tsx and widened the main content max-width (max-w-3xl -> max-w-5xl) since the 3/4-column grids needed more room than the old single-column screens.
2026-07-14: npm run build and npm run lint both clean.

2026-07-14: Playwright walkthrough against live uvicorn + next dev (localhost origin, per feat-015's CORS finding): loaded /dashboard, confirmed all 5 section headings render, zero console/page errors. Screenshot cross-checked field-by-field against the direct GET /dashboard/summary curl captured during feat-013's verification: farm_health_score 70 (matches (90+90+10+90)/4 exactly), active alerts 1 (FP-001 critical, correct notes text), tasks due today 7 (9 total pending recommendations from feat-012's test runs minus the 2 approved/rejected in feat-013 = 7, correct), weather fields exact match (27.0C/91%/11.8mm/15km-h), daily recommendations correctly showing the top 5 sorted by priority-then-confidence (93/93/85/85/78, matching feat-013's verified sort order), asset status overview showing all 4 assets with correct health scores/status/colors.
features.notes: RecommendationCard is now shared and will be reused as-is (or with an added approve/reject action slot via its children prop) by feat-017's asset detail screen and feat-018's AI Copilot panel.

features.name: Asset detail screen
features.description: Build Screen 3 per docs/ui-build-plan.md: simulated sensor values (type-specific, labeled 'simulated'), AI analysis narrative, one recommendation card per pending recommendation (full 6-field format with Approve/Reject), today's tasks, prediction, and history. Reads GET /assets/{id} and GET /assets/{id}/recommendations; writes via POST /recommendations/{id}/approve|reject.
features.user_visible_behavior: Clicking an asset on the digital twin or dashboard opens a detail view showing that asset's real sensor readings, AI reasoning, and actionable recommendation cards; approving/rejecting one updates the panel in place.
verification: npm run build && npm run lint succeed in frontend/

Manual/Playwright walkthrough: open one asset of each type, confirm type-specific fields render correctly, approve one recommendation and confirm the panel updates and Snowflake's RECOMMENDATIONS row actually flips status (cross-check via curl)
evidence: 2026-07-14: Picked up frontend/app/assets/[id]/page.tsx and the ApiError class in frontend/lib/api.ts, which existed uncommitted in the working tree from an interrupted prior session. Removed leftover DEBUG console.log statements in load(). Found and deleted 3 leftover 'TEST ... race-condition debugging' RECOMMENDATIONS rows in the live Snowflake account (recommendation_ids containing 'TEST') left over from that interrupted session -- these were polluting real demo data, not legitimate seed/generated content.
2026-07-14: npm run build and npm run lint both clean.

2026-07-14: POST /workflow/run against the live account (91.9s) generated 4 fresh real RECOMMENDATIONS rows for FP-001 (still in its critical DO crisis). Playwright (chromium, scratchpad install) walkthrough against live uvicorn + next dev (localhost origin, per feat-015's known CORS finding): home page -> click FP-001 marker -> navigated to /assets/FP-001 correctly. Sensor readings showed real fish-pond values (water temp 33.64C, pH 6.93, DO 2.0 mg/L, feed 30.53%, biomass 324.1 kg). AI analysis showed a real CRITICAL badge + dissolved-oxygen notes. Prediction card present. Exactly 4 recommendation cards rendered, each with real Recommendation/Reason/Evidence/Expected-impact/Confidence text and Approve/Reject buttons. Today's tasks listed all 4. History showed 3 real entries.

2026-07-14: Found and fixed a real bug during the approve/reject walkthrough: handleDecision() called load() without awaiting it, then immediately cleared pendingId in a finally block -- this re-enabled the Approve/Reject buttons on the stale (pre-refresh) card during the ~4-5s Snowflake round-trip for the refetch, before the UI had actually updated. A first walkthrough pass (by a sub-agent) misread this as 'approve does nothing' and rapid-double-clicked, ending up approving all 4 real FP-001 recommendations at once as an unintended side effect. Reproduced independently with a scripted Playwright click-and-poll test (confirmed the POST succeeds and the refetch GETs return correct data, but the button stayed clickable through the gap). Fixed by making load() return its promise and awaiting it in handleDecision before clearing pendingId. Re-verified with the same script: button now stays disabled==true for the entire refresh window, and a second scripted run confirmed a real approve (2->1 pending) and a real reject (1->0 pending) each worked correctly with zero console errors.

2026-07-14: Found and fixed a second real bug: READING_FIELDS_BY_TYPE in page.tsx didn't match asset_simulator.py's actual per-type fields (confirmed against both the simulator's _NUMERIC_METRICS/next_reading and docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md's 'Simulated Data' section, which lists Fish/Chicken/Rice/Fruit-Orchard fields explicitly). chicken_coop was missing water_l (a real chicken metric); rice_field wrongly listed water_l (a chicken-only field, always null for rice -- copy-paste leftover); fruit_orchard was missing growth_stage (which the simulator does generate for fruit_orchard, next_reading() line 125-126) and wrongly listed air_temp_c/humidity_pct (chicken-only fields, always null for orchard). Fixed all three lists to match the vision doc + simulator exactly.

2026-07-14: Re-verified live after the fix: npm run build/npm run lint clean again. A scripted Playwright pass loaded all 4 asset ids and confirmed the exact expected field-label set per type (CC-001: Eggs today/Feed level/Air temp/Humidity/Water volume; FO-001: Growth stage/Soil moisture/Disease risk/Harvest readiness; RF-001: Growth stage/Soil moisture/Nitrogen/Irrigation; FP-001: Water temp/pH/Dissolved oxygen/Feed level/Biomass) with zero '—' placeholder dashes -- every displayed field now has a real backend-populated value (e.g. FO-001 correctly showed 'harvest ready' as its real growth stage, consistent with feat-014's evidence of the orchard's harvest-ready recommendation).
2026-07-14: Re-verified /assets/DOES-NOT-EXIST still renders the clean not-found message with zero console errors after both fixes.
2026-07-14: Stopped both uvicorn and next dev processes cleanly after verification.
features.notes: Direct replacement for the old Screen 2 (plots/[id]/page.tsx), generalized across 4 asset types instead of one crop type. Two real bugs found and fixed during this session's verification (button re-enabled during refresh race; wrong per-type sensor field lists) -- see evidence for root causes. FP-001's recommendation backlog was fully drained (all approved/rejected) as a side effect of testing the approve/reject flow live against the real account; the next /workflow/run call will regenerate fresh ones since the pond is still critical.

features.name: AI Copilot panel
features.description: Build Screen 4 per docs/ui-build-plan.md: a persistent, prominent surface (side panel or dedicated route) showing a prioritized cross-asset recommendation feed plus a free-form question box wired to POST /copilot/ask. This is the vision doc's explicit centerpiece requirement -- 'the application should never feel like a dashboard with a chatbot attached.'
features.user_visible_behavior: A farmer can type 'What should I do today?' or click through the example questions from docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md and get a grounded, actionable answer, from any screen in the app.
verification: npm run build && npm run lint succeed in frontend/
Manual/Playwright walkthrough: from at least 2 different screens, open the copilot, ask 2+ of the vision doc's example questions, confirm grounded (not generic) answers render
evidence: 2026-07-14: Decided the open layout question: a persistent floating-action-button + slide-over panel (frontend/components/CopilotPanel.tsx), mounted once in frontend/app/layout.tsx (the root layout, which App Router does not remount on client-side navigation) rather than a dedicated route -- this makes it a true always-available surface on every screen with conversation state that survives navigating between screens, not a per-page widget. Panel has two sections: 'Today's priorities' (farm-wide, priority-sorted recommendation feed reusing RecommendationCard, sourced from GET /dashboard/summary's top_recommendations, read-only with a 'View asset' link -- matches the existing Dashboard screen's read-only usage of the same component, no duplicate approve/reject logic added) and 'Ask a question' (free-form input wired to POST /copilot/ask, chat-style exchange history, 3 example-question quick-select chips from the vision doc).
2026-07-14: npm run build and npm run lint both clean.

2026-07-14: Playwright walkthrough against live uvicorn + next dev (localhost origin). From the home page (/): opened the panel, confirmed the empty-state 'No pending recommendations right now.' message when 0 recommendations were pending (real state at the time, drained during feat-017's live approve/reject testing) -- not a crash. Asked 'What should I do today?' -- got a real, grounded, multi-asset answer (FP-001 critical DO crisis with full 6-field recommendations citing exact sensor values and the Q4-2024 history; FO-001 correctly flagged harvest-ready at 92.92% readiness; CC-001/RF-001 correctly called healthy with real supporting numbers) ending with a concrete next step. Closed the panel, used a real client-side nav-link click (not page.goto, which would trigger a full reload and unfairly reset state) to /dashboard, reopened the panel, and confirmed the prior Q&A exchange was still present -- proves the panel is a genuinely persistent cross-screen surface, not per-page state. Asked 'Should I feed the fish?' from the dashboard screen -- got a real grounded 'No' answer citing the same live DO/temp values and Q4-2024 comparison, ending with a concrete next step. Both answers cleared the vision doc's grounded-vs-generic bar (same bar feat-014 was held to).

2026-07-14: Triggered a fresh POST /workflow/run (77.4s) to generate 5 new real pending recommendations for FP-001, then re-verified the 'Today's priorities' section with the populated case: confirmed all 5 real RecommendationCard instances rendered (matching GET /dashboard/summary's top_recommendations exactly), each with real Recommendation/Reason/Evidence/Priority/Expected-impact/Confidence content and a working 'View asset' link. (First 2s-wait check misread this as 0 cards -- a test-script timing issue on the live Snowflake round-trip, not a real bug; confirmed by rechecking with a longer wait and by directly inspecting the fetched dashboard/summary response.)
2026-07-14: Zero console/page errors across every step of both walkthrough passes. Stopped uvicorn and next dev cleanly after verification.
features.notes: This feature is what separates FarmTwin from a monitoring dashboard per the vision doc -- treated its verification bar as strict, not perfunctory. Mounting in the root layout (rather than a dedicated /copilot route) was the key design decision -- it's what makes 'persistent surface' literally true (conversation state survives navigation) rather than just a stylistic claim. The priorities feed is intentionally read-only (View-asset link only, no inline approve/reject) to avoid duplicating feat-017's action-handling logic in a second place -- matches the existing Dashboard screen's precedent for the same shared component.
